perturbation.original_text: 1.4. Confidential Information shall mean, subject to the provisions of Section 9.2 [Exclusions], all proprietary and confidential information of a Party, including, without limitation, trade secrets, technical information, business information, sales information, customer and potential customer lists and identities, product sales plans, sublicense agreements, inventions, developments, discoveries, software, know-how, methods, techniques, formulae, data, processes and other proprietary ideas, whether or not protectable under patent, trademark, copyright or other areas of law, that the other Party has access to or receives. For purposes of this Agreement, this Agreement shall be considered Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.4. Confidential Information shall mean, subject to the provisions of Section 9.2 [Exclusions], all proprietary and confidential information of a Party, including, without limitation, trade secrets, technical information, business information, sales information, customer and potential customer lists and identities, product sales plans, sublicense agreements, inventions, developments, discoveries, software, know-how, methods, techniques, formulae, data, processes and other proprietary ideas, whether or not protectable under patent, trademark, copyright or other areas of law, that the other Party has access to or receives. For purposes of this Agreement, this Agreement shall *NOT* be considered Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the definition of 'Confidential Information' in Section 1.4 to explicitly exclude 'this Agreement' from being considered Confidential Information, the contract introduces a direct contradiction with Section 9.1, which presumes this Agreement is considered Confidential Information, therefore creating an in-text contradiction. This creates uncertainty regarding the confidentiality obligations surrounding the agreement itself.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9.1. Confidentiality Obligations. Except as permitted elsewhere under this Agreement, each Party agrees to take Reasonable Steps (as defined below) (a) to receive and maintain the Confidential Information of the other Party in confidence and (b) not to disclose such Confidential Information to any third parties, provided, the receiving Party may disclose such Confidential Information to its employees, representatives and agents who have a need to know such information for purposes of carrying out the terms of this Agreement. Neither Party hereto shall use all or any part of the Confidential Information of the other Party for any purpose other than to perform its obligations under this Agreement. The Parties will take Reasonable Steps (as defined below) to ensure that their employees, representatives and agents comply with this provision. As used herein, "Reasonable Steps" means at least the same degree of care that the receiving Party uses to protect its own Confidential Information, and, in any event, no less than reasonable care.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that 'this Agreement shall NOT be considered Confidential Information', while the contradicted text (Section 9.1) imposes confidentiality obligations on 'Confidential Information', directly implying that the agreement IS considered confidential. This creates a direct and undeniable conflict, making it unclear whether the agreement itself is subject to the confidentiality terms outlined in Section 9.1. This qualifies as a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 1.20. Term shall mean the Effective Date through June 15, 2001 and any Renewal Term (as defined in paragraph 7.4 herein.)
perturbation.changed_text: 1.20. Term shall mean the Effective Date through June 15, 2001, *unless terminated earlier as per Section 7.1, irrespective of any Renewal Term* (as defined in paragraph 7.4 herein.)
perturbation.explanation: By adding the clause "unless terminated earlier as per Section 7.1, irrespective of any Renewal Term" to the definition of 'Term' in Section 1.20, it creates a contradiction with Section 7.4 regarding the automatic extension of the contract into a Renewal Term. While Section 1.20 now suggests that the agreement can always be terminated as per section 7.1, Section 7.4 gives LeadersOnline the option to extend the Term of the agreement for an additional 12 months, with a clause suggesting that even with an extension the agreement can be terminated.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.4. LeadersOnline shall have the option, subject to VerticalNet's approval exercised in its sole and absolute discretion, to extend the Term of this Agreement for an additional 12 months (a "Renewal Term") on such terms and conditions as may be mutually agreed upon by the Parties. To exercise this option, LeadersOnline must notify VerticalNet in writing of its election no later than 90 days prior to the expiration of the initial Term. Unless VerticalNet notifies LeadersOnline of its intention to extend the initial Term of this Agreement for an additional year within 30 days of receiving such notice the Agreement shall terminate in accordance with the terms hereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a clause to the definition of 'Term' allowing for earlier termination under Section 7.1, 'irrespective of any Renewal Term'. This directly contradicts Section 7.4, which gives LeadersOnline the option to extend the term for an additional 12 months (Renewal Term) subject to VerticalNet's approval. The addition of 'irrespective of any Renewal Term' means that Section 7.1 can always override any renewal term. This creates a clear contradiction because Section 7.4 establishes a process for extending the term, while the modification to 1.20 allows that extension to be terminated early under different conditions than those described in section 7.4, and against the renewal term. Therefore a renewal could be triggered, but immediately ended by Section 7.1 making the renewal option pointless. This is a structural flaw.

perturbation.original_text: 6.5. Guaranteed Revenues. VerticalNet agrees to guarantee that, during the initial Term of this Agreement, LeadersOnline shall earn LeadersOnline- VerticalNet Revenue of at least *** Dollars ($***) (the "Guaranteed Amount"), subject to the provisions of this Section 6.5 [Guaranteed Revenues]. *** Within thirty (30) days after the end of the initial Term, LeadersOnline shall submit a statement showing a reasonably detailed accounting of the LeadersOnline-VerticalNet Revenue received during the initial Term (or earned during the initial Term and paid within 30 days thereafter) along with payment of all such amounts up to the Guaranteed Amount. In the event this Agreement is terminated in accordance with Section 7.1 [DEFINITIONS], LeadersOnline shall make a payment of all amounts of LeadersOnline- VerticalNet Revenues received through the date of termination, up to the amount of the Guaranteed Amount ***.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.5. Guaranteed Revenues. VerticalNet agrees to guarantee that, during the initial Term of this Agreement, LeadersOnline shall earn LeadersOnline- VerticalNet Revenue of at least *** Dollars ($***) (the "Guaranteed Amount"), subject to the provisions of this Section 6.5 [Guaranteed Revenues]. *** Within thirty (30) days after the end of the initial Term, LeadersOnline shall submit a statement showing a reasonably detailed accounting of the LeadersOnline-VerticalNet Revenue received during the initial Term (or earned during the initial Term and paid within 30 days thereafter) along with payment of all such amounts up to the Guaranteed Amount. *Regardless of termination under section 7.1, LeadersOnline shall not be responsible for any payments or accounting related to the Guaranteed Amount* In the event this Agreement is terminated in accordance with Section 7.1 [DEFINITIONS], LeadersOnline shall make a payment of all amounts of LeadersOnline- VerticalNet Revenues received through the date of termination, up to the amount of the Guaranteed Amount ***.
perturbation.explanation: By inserting "Regardless of termination under section 7.1, LeadersOnline shall not be responsible for any payments or accounting related to the Guaranteed Amount" into Section 6.5, which discusses guaranteed revenues, there is a conflict created. This statement directly contradicts the subsequent sentence within the same section that specifies, 'In the event this Agreement is terminated in accordance with Section 7.1 [DEFINITIONS], LeadersOnline shall make a payment of all amounts of LeadersOnline- VerticalNet Revenues received through the date of termination, up to the amount of the Guaranteed Amount'. This introduces uncertainty regarding LeadersOnline’s obligation to meet the guaranteed revenue target upon early termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.5. Guaranteed Revenues. VerticalNet agrees to guarantee that, during the initial Term of this Agreement, LeadersOnline shall earn LeadersOnline- VerticalNet Revenue of at least *** Dollars ($***) (the "Guaranteed Amount"), subject to the provisions of this Section 6.5 [Guaranteed Revenues]. *** Within thirty (30) days after the end of the initial Term, LeadersOnline shall submit a statement showing a reasonably detailed accounting of the LeadersOnline-VerticalNet Revenue received during the initial Term (or earned during the initial Term and paid within 30 days thereafter) along with payment of all such amounts up to the Guaranteed Amount. Regardless of termination under section 7.1, LeadersOnline shall not be responsible for any payments or accounting related to the Guaranteed Amount In the event this Agreement is terminated in accordance with Section 7.1 [DEFINITIONS], LeadersOnline shall make a payment of all amounts of LeadersOnline- VerticalNet Revenues received through the date of termination, up to the amount of the Guaranteed Amount ***.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction within section 6.5. The added sentence, 'Regardless of termination under section 7.1, LeadersOnline shall not be responsible for any payments or accounting related to the Guaranteed Amount,' directly conflicts with the subsequent sentence in the same section, which states, 'In the event this Agreement is terminated in accordance with Section 7.1 [DEFINITIONS], LeadersOnline shall make a payment of all amounts of LeadersOnline- VerticalNet Revenues received through the date of termination, up to the amount of the Guaranteed Amount.' This creates an irreconcilable inconsistency regarding LeadersOnline's financial obligations upon termination under section 7.1.
